title: Journal bearing assembly with drainage facilitation element
abstract: A journal bearing assembly (100) for use in a turbomachine includes a bearing housing (500, 600, 700) configured to receive a rotor shaft, the bearing housing (500, 600, 700) comprising one or more drain outlets (582, 584, 682) for draining lubricating fluid utilized during rotation of the rotor shaft, and a drainage facilitation element (520, 522, 620, 730) positioned on an inner surface of the bearing housing (500, 600, 700), wherein the drainage facilitation element (520, 522, 620, 730) is configured to obstruct and redirect the lubricating fluid towards the at least one drain outlet (582, 584, 682) so that the lubricating fluid drains naturally due to loss of kinetic energy in the at least one drain outlet (582, 584, 682).
claims: Claims 1. A journal bearing assembly (100) for use in a turbomachine comprising: a bearing housing (500, 600, 700) configured to receive a rotor shaft, the bearing housing (500, 600, 700) comprising at least one drain outlet (582, 584, 682) for draining lubricating fluid utilized during rotation of the rotor shaft, and a drainage facilitation element (520, 522, 620, 730) positioned on an inner surface of the bearing housing (500, 600, 700), wherein the drainage facilitation element (520, 522, 620, 730) is configured to obstruct and redirect the lubricating fluid towards the at least one drain outlet (582, 584, 682) so that the lubricating fluid drains naturally due to loss of kinetic energy in the at least one drain outlet (582, 584, 682). 2. The journal bearing assembly (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein a residence volume of the lubricating fluid in the bearing housing (500, 600, 700) is minimized by the drainage facilitation element (520, 522, 620, 730) during operation of the journal bearing assembly (100). 3. The journal bearing assembly (100) as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the drainage facilitation element comprises vertical walls (520, 522) positioned adjacent to the at least one drain outlet (582, 584) on an inner surface of the bearing housing (500). 4. The journal bearing assembly (100) as claimed in claim 3, wherein the vertical walls (520, 522) are arranged in parallel to an axial direction (530) of the bearing housing (500). 5. The journal bearing assembly (100) as claimed in claim 3, further comprising a gap (590) between the vertical walls (520, 522) and bearing housing end walls to allow lubricating fluid flow towards the at least one drain outlet (582, 584). 6. The journal bearing assembly (100) as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the drainage facilitation element comprises a scoop element (620) positioned on an inner surface of the bearing housing (600). 7. The journal bearing assembly (100) as claimed in claim 6, wherein the scoop element (620) is situated above the at least one drain outlet (682) in direction of a lubricating fluid flow. 8. The journal bearing assembly (100) as claimed in claim 6 or 7, wherein the scoop element (620) comprises a width (W) and a height (H), and wherein a width to height ratio is approximately four (W/H = ~ 4). 9. The journal bearing assembly (100) as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the bearing housing (700) comprises opposite end walls (714, 716), and wherein the drainage facilitation element comprises a circular inner liner (730) located inside the bearing housing (700) on an outer section of an end wall (716). 10. The journal bearing assembly (100) as claimed in claim 9, wherein the inner liner (730) is arranged to provide an approximate vertical symmetry of the opposite end walls (714, 716). 11. A turbomachine comprising: a journal bearing assembly (100) comprising: a bearing housing (500, 600, 700) configured to receive a rotor shaft, the bearing housing (500, 600, 700) comprising at least one drain outlet (582, 584, 682) for draining lubricating fluid utilized during rotation of the rotor shaft, and a drainage facilitation element (520, 522, 620, 730) positioned on an inner surface of the bearing housing (500, 600, 700), wherein the drainage facilitation element (520, 522, 620, 730) is configured to obstruct and redirect the lubricating fluid towards the at least one drain outlet (582, 584, 682) so that the lubricating fluid drains naturally due to loss of kinetic energy in the at least one drain outlet (582, 584, 682). 12. The turbomachine of claim 11 , wherein the journal bearing assembly (100) is arranged between a turbine section (150) and a compressor section (140). 13. The turbomachine as claimed in claim 11, comprising multiple journal bearing assemblies (100). 14. The turbomachine as claimed in claim 11 , configured as a gas turbine. 15. The turbomachine as claimed in any of the preceding claims 11 to 14, wherein a residence volume of the lubricating fluid in the bearing housing (500, 600, 700) is minimized by the drainage facilitation element (520, 522, 620, 730) during operation of the turbomachine.
